title: GoalOS Signoff Pro — Autonomous Site Navigator & Redirect Console

publicSafe.boundary: The navigator runs in the visitor's browser, loads only static same-origin JSON, does not call an LLM API, and never requires personal or confidential data.

releaseFacts.title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension v4.4.0 — Ethereum Mainnet Deployment
releaseFacts.claimBoundary: This public UI is read-only and educational. It does not request wallets, move funds, approve tokens, broadcast transactions, provide legal/investment advice, or authorize production use.

boundary: Static GitHub Pages implementation. Browser-local deterministic answer routing by default. No external AI calls, no wallet, no uploads, no analytics, no payments, zero value moved.

roles.label: AI consultant / agency
roles.defaultQuestion: How do I create a client signoff?

roles.label: Client reviewer
roles.defaultQuestion: How do I know whether AI work is done?

roles.label: DAO / treasury council
roles.defaultQuestion: How do we require a proof package before payment?

roles.label: Protocol operator
roles.defaultQuestion: How do the 48 contracts support Signoff?

roles.label: Auditor / reviewer
roles.defaultQuestion: Where do I inspect evidence, replay, and claim boundaries?

roles.label: RSI / ASI governance team
roles.defaultQuestion: How does the proof loop connect to RSI and ASI governance?

quickQuestions: How do I know when AI work is actually done?

topics.title: Start here
topics.answer: Start with the command center. It asks who you are, explains the simplest next step, and routes you to the right proof lab or product console.

topics.title: GoalOS Signoff
keywords: know when ai work is done
topics.answer: GoalOS Signoff is the customer-facing product: define done, submit evidence, check completeness, obtain human approval, and issue a Mission Receipt.

topics.title: AI Research & Strategy Signoff
topics.answer: v37 is the product-first console. It shows the five-step experience: Create → Submit → Check → Review → Receipt.

topics.title: Browser beta
topics.answer: The browser beta is the safest demo path: it runs locally, needs no wallet, moves no value, and lets visitors inspect the proof cycle without giving data.

topics.title: Mission 001
topics.answer: Mission 001 is the benchmark-ready reproducibility packet: mission contract, environment, baselines, proof bundle, replay log, ledgers, validator report, scoreboard, and claims matrix.

topics.title: All public labs
topics.answer: Open the public lab catalog to see the full GoalOS arc: proof loop, blockchain credibility, Proof Before Settlement, RSI/ASI governance, product-first Signoff, and the 48-contract atlas.

topics.title: 48 Mainnet contract atlas
topics.answer: The 48-contract atlas explains the GoalOS-created Ethereum Mainnet rails. The release records 48 GoalOS-created contracts, 48 / 48 verified Etherscan source verification, and a configured but not production-activated boundary.

topics.title: Individual contract pages
topics.answer: Open the contract index to inspect every GoalOS-created Mainnet contract, grouped by family, role, Signoff tier, proof stage, address, and Etherscan link.

topics.title: Signoff Basic
topics.answer: Signoff Basic is the acquisition product: no wallet, no chain transaction, no token requirement, but produces a protocol-compatible Evidence Docket and Mission Receipt.

topics.title: Signoff Verified
topics.answer: Signoff Verified adds hashes, proof cards, credentials, replay references, and protocol-compatible evidence records. It should not require the customer to handle token approvals.

topics.title: Signoff Secured
topics.answer: Signoff Secured adds accountable review: reviewer/evaluator bonds, challenge windows, reputation, claim boundaries, falsification, and appeal rails after audit and authorization gates.

topics.title: Signoff & Settle
topics.answer: Signoff & Settle is the advanced tier where accepted work can trigger bounded rewards, escrow, reputation, dispute handling, and settlement rails after production authorization.

topics.title: $AGIALPHA role
topics.answer: $AGIALPHA is not the Signoff product. It is the optional economic security and settlement asset: fees, bonds, reviewer stakes, challenge stakes, work rewards, and protocol revenue where authorized.

topics.title: Production boundary
topics.answer: The public site is educational and read-only. It does not authorize user funds, production activation, token approvals, or live settlement. The Mainnet release is configured and verified, not a public-fund activation.

topics.title: Proof Before Settlement
topics.answer: Proof Before Settlement formalizes the core standard: blockchain proves the transaction; GoalOS proves the work. Evidence, validation, receipt, and claim boundaries come before settlement readiness.

topics.title: From Loop to RSI
keywords: target emit filter atlas test plan eval insert promote
topics.answer: The RSI arc upgrades proof-gated work into governed invention: deterministic pipeline, schema-bound artifacts, baseline comparisons, Move-37 dossiers, and mechanical gates.

topics.title: ASI Mission Simulator
topics.answer: The v35 Mission Simulator lets visitors run the Loop → RSI → ASI path through gates, scenarios, dossiers, council review, rollback, and synthetic receipt logic.

topics.title: AGIALPHA 48-Contract Atlas
topics.answer: v38 is the contract atlas: a friendly interface over the 48 GoalOS-created Mainnet contracts, mapping each rail to product tiers and proof-to-settlement stages.

topics.title: Ask GoalOS Navigator
topics.answer: v39 adds the autonomous site navigator: ask a question, receive a browser-local answer, and get routed to the best page or proof object.

topics.title: Capital, vaults & treasury
topics.answer: Capital, vaults & treasury contains 6 GoalOS-created Mainnet contracts: CommercializationPerformanceVault, ProofRewardsVault, LiquidityVault, SecurityVault, CommunityVault, TreasuryRouter. Open the atlas to filter the full family and inspect individual contract pages.

topics.title: Core proof, work & protocol registries
topics.answer: Core proof, work & protocol registries contains 13 GoalOS-created Mainnet contracts: ProofSeedRegistry, LegacyAGIJobManagerRegistry, ReputationRegistry, ReferralRegistry, ProofCardRegistry, ProofCredentialRegistry, JobRegistry, JobClaimBondManager…. Open the atlas to filter the full family and inspect individual contract pages.

topics.title: Dispute, participation & credential governance
topics.answer: Dispute, participation & credential governance contains 5 GoalOS-created Mainnet contracts: DisputeRegistry, AppealRegistry, SponsorRegistry, BuilderProfileRegistry, CredentialRevocationRegistry. Open the atlas to filter the full family and inspect individual contract pages.

topics.title: AEP execution, evidence & rollout architecture
topics.answer: AEP execution, evidence & rollout architecture contains 15 GoalOS-created Mainnet contracts: AEPAgentRegistry, AEPArtifactRegistry, AEPGoalOSCommitRegistry, AEPRunCommitmentRegistry, AEPProofLedger, AEPEvalRegistry, AEPAttestationRegistry, AEPSelectionGate…. Open the atlas to filter the full family and inspect individual contract pages.

topics.title: Institutional assurance, staking & accountability
topics.answer: Institutional assurance, staking & accountability contains 9 GoalOS-created Mainnet contracts: AEPConformanceRegistry, AEPClaimBoundaryRegistry, AEPReplayRegistry, AEPCommitRevealValidationRegistry, AEPEvaluatorStakingRegistry, AEPSlashingCourt, AEPRewardVault, AEPChronicleRegistry…. Open the atlas to filter the full family and inspect individual contract pages.

topics.title: CommercializationPerformanceVault
keywords: Receives and tracks commercialization-performance capital as a vault rail.
topics.answer: CommercializationPerformanceVault is a Capital, vaults & treasury rail for Commercialization reserve. In Signoff, it belongs mainly to the Settle layer and supports Settle. Open its page for address, Etherscan link, role, tier, and public-safe boundary.

topics.title: ProofRewardsVault
keywords: Holds reward capital for proof work, reviewers, and accepted work flows.
topics.answer: ProofRewardsVault is a Capital, vaults & treasury rail for Proof reward reserve. In Signoff, it belongs mainly to the Settle layer and supports Settle. Open its page for address, Etherscan link, role, tier, and public-safe boundary.

topics.answer: LiquidityVault is a Capital, vaults & treasury rail for Liquidity reserve. In Signoff, it belongs mainly to the Settle layer and supports Protocol support. Open its page for address, Etherscan link, role, tier, and public-safe boundary.

topics.answer: SecurityVault is a Capital, vaults & treasury rail for Security reserve. In Signoff, it belongs mainly to the Secured layer and supports Protocol support. Open its page for address, Etherscan link, role, tier, and public-safe boundary.

topics.title: CommunityVault

topics.answer: CommunityVault is a Capital, vaults & treasury rail for Community reserve. In Signoff, it belongs mainly to the Settle layer and supports Protocol support. Open its page for address, Etherscan link, role, tier, and public-safe boundary.
